_doc: Release build manifest for the Tepna Dex suite. GATE A in verify-provenance.html asserts each bundle's CURRENT manifestHash == the value here, forcing a re-bundle whenever ANY external *.js/CSS module changes (closes the build-provenance drift hole that buildHash alone misses — external-review #7 / REVIEW-FOLLOWUP P0). manifestHash = SHA-256[0:12] of a UUID-INDEPENDENT projection of the bundle's __bundler/manifest (drop the inliner's fresh-per-build random UUID keys, gunzip each asset, hash the decompressed bytes, tag mime+compressed, sort, hash the join) — a pure function of the inlined executed code that is STABLE across re-bundles of identical source and moves ONLY on a real JS/CSS change (PROVENANCE-NONDETERMINISM-2026-06-29 §1; the prior raw-inner-text hash drifted on EVERY re-bundle because of the UUID keys). Canonical algorithm = verify-provenance.html manifestHashOf() (now single-sourced in manifest-gate.js manifestHashFromText, shared with the Node sibling tests/verify-manifest.mjs). Recompute each manifestHash with that projection and hand-update the matching entry after any re-bundle. NOTE (SIGNAL-ADAPTER-AND-FRONTIER Phase 7, 2026-06-30): the per-bundle `buildHash` field below is RETIRED as a provenance signal — verify-provenance.html no longer boots bundles or reads buildHash at all (the gate is pure-static; GATE A = manifestHash only, GATE B is the content-addressed known-answer audit in FIXTURE-PROVENANCE.json). The buildHash values are kept ONLY as inert legacy metadata for historical continuity (ganglior-provenance.js still stamps buildHash into exports, but no gate reads it; it was NOT re-bundled away because that would churn every fixture for zero gate value). Do NOT rely on buildHash for anything; manifestHash is the sole executed-code identity.
_note_ppgdex_beatdetection_perf: PPGDEX-BEAT-DETECTION-PERF-AND-HRV-FIDELITY-2026-07-02-BRIEF executed 2026-07-02. ppgdex-dsp.js (behavior): §1 detectBeats REPLACED the O(N·lag) whole-record autocorrelation + global-period segment scan with an O(N) TERMA detector (Elgendi 2013 — positive-slope upstroke energy, dual moving-average LOCAL adaptive threshold, no global period), run as a 3-LED CONSENSUS: detectChannel detects ch0/ch1/ch2 independently, consensusBeats keeps a beat only where >=2 of 3 place a systolic peak within +-50 ms (optical bSQI) and DROPS single-LED beats (never median-fills). §2 pickChannel/channelSNR demoted to a windowed ~90 s SNR ranking (reference channel only; all 3 detected). §2b detectChannelsAsync Web-Worker pool (inlined blob URL, 100% offline, byte-identical serial fallback — analyze()/compute() stay SYNCHRONOUS; only the app awaits it + stashes rec._preChannels). §3 whole-record HRV carries hrvLowConfidence (+reason) when analyzable<60 % OR correction>20 %. §4 rMSSD/pNN50/SD1 computed over the SQI-clean adjacent-pair subset (PPI_ECTOPY_THR unchanged). ppgdex-render.js/-app.js/-registry.js: §5 3-LED agreement folded into beatSQI + per-epoch & whole-record ledAgreementPct + a stacked agreement ribbon (measured badge). REAL re-bundle (behavior changed): PpgDex.html manifestHash 0e0d255186c7->d5fa185f6f97. FIXTURE-PROVENANCE PpgDex equiv fixture REGENERATED (recording.contentId 8810d84e3c4b->7e887548f693; outputHash f6c08a7ffb421fb9->69131ebdfe4d8ff1) — on the committed 6.5-min clip: 338 consensus beats, rMSSD 36.6 ms, correction 1.5 %, analyzable 96 %, LED agreement 95 %. _note_node_residue_followups_ii: NODE-RESIDUE-FOLLOWUPS-II-2026-06-30-BRIEF executed 2026-07-02 (cheap closeout — decisions + doc/comment annotations, NO behavior change). §1 (DECIDE): ECGDex has NO symmetric categorical SQI floor — INTENTIONAL, not an oversight. ECGDex autonomic_surge events ALSO carry a per-event sqi and effConf already tapers them proportionally in the apnea noisy-OR, but the categorical floor stays PpgDex-ONLY on purpose: PpgDex is limb-worn OPTICAL (Polar Verity Sense, motion-prone, sqi legitimately dips into the unusable tail so the extra distrust is warranted) while ECGDex is a CHEST STRAP (Polar H10, sqi rarely reaches < PPG_SQI_FLOOR on a real recording so a floor would almost never fire; effConf's smooth taper suffices). Deliberately NOT generalized to a shared NODE_SQI_FLOOR table (per-node table + shared post-map + a test twin = surface area for a floor that would essentially never fire on chest-strap data). Documented at the PpgDex-branch floor site in integrator-dsp.js + EVENT-LEXICON §6.10. §2 (VERIFY→DECIDE): the sqiFloor (PpgDex) + clampFloor (GlucoDex) INGESTED tags are AUDIT-ONLY — grep-confirmed NOT read anywhere (integrator-dsp.js / -render.js / -app.js; not surfaced in buildFusionExport finding sources[] nor on any render card); the conf ×0.5 each floor applies is the LOAD-BEARING down-weight (flows through effConf → noisy-OR → posterior). Annotated beside BOTH stamps (mirrors the documented meta.derived audit-only precedent) + EVENT-LEXICON §6.10. §3/§4/§5 no-action per recorded rationale (the floor firing on non-fused PpgDex impulses is inert — only autonomic_surge is gathered by fusion; PPG_SQI_FLOOR/×0.5 calibration deferred to a labeled apnea corpus; Node-CI is env standing debt). COMMENT-ONLY integrator-dsp.js edit → Integrator re-bundled fe4c2c623820→be7e7aa83355; buildHash 78e04e861cce UNCHANGED (external-JS comment, no inline-script/style .src.html shell edit) → GATE A updated below. No test change — decisions/comments only; the existing NODE-RESIDUE §3 sqi-floor group still covers the loop. Other 7 bundles UNTOUCHED.

_note_synth_texture_followups: SYNTH-TEXTURE-FOLLOWUPS-2026-06-24-BRIEF executed 2026-06-24 (the spun-out DSP audit). Item 1 (FIX): pulsedex-dsp.js lombScargle now tracks the GLOBAL spectral peak across the whole analysed band (gPeakF/gPeakP) and returns peakHz/peakBand/peakBelowHF/respBelowHF — the old code tracked the peak ONLY inside the HF branch so a dominant sub-HF oscillation (the corpus CSR at ~0.017 Hz) was invisible; respRate stays HF-derived (back-compat). pulsedex-render.js Resp Rate row now caveats (warn + note) when the robust whole-night periodicBreathingIndex flags periodic breathing. Item 2 (verify + defensive): pulsedex-dsp.js sampEn gained an inert O(N²) length cap (MAXN=20000, deterministic decimation) — audit found the sole caller (pulsedex-app.js) always passes a bounded series (5-min representative window for long recs; <90-min short rec otherwise), so the cap never triggers today; it only bounds a future full-night caller. Item 3 (verify, NO fix): cleanArtifactHR (oxydex-dsp.js) runs first (processNight ~line 1499), feeding all detection — correct by design (artifacts removed pre-detection); the ≥20 bpm/1-sample HARD criterion targets the instantaneous O2Ring-S firmware step, NOT physiological arousal-rebound tachy (synthetic night-2, most-aroused, has 0 samples with ≥20 bpm 1-sample jumps; max step 4 bpm) — arousals survive into CVHR/PB detection. _note_ecg_rpeak_seedfix: ECG-RPEAK-SEED-FIX-2026-06-27 executed 2026-06-27. ecgdex-dsp.js detectPeaks: the Pan-Tompkins integrate threshold is now seeded from a ROBUST GLOBAL scale (new _seedScale: a subsampled ~99th-pct of the whole-record integrate, ≈ strong-QRS level) instead of max(first 2 s). A recording that opened mid electrode-settling (a multi-kµV transient ≫ the real QRS) seeded SPKI/THRI ~10-20× too high; SPKI only decays when a peak FIRES, so it never recovered → <12 peaks → a false 'signal may be flat' throw on an otherwise-good night (the user's real Polar_H10_..._20260625_215300_ECG.txt). The global pct is behaviour-preserving on clean records (the seed ≈ the old max-of-2s) but a ≤2 s startup transient is now a negligible fraction of the night.
